`@src/content/posts/2026-04_認証と認可 Keycloak入門 第2版.md` の `## 目次` をMarkdown形式に変換しました。

- 「■入門編■ / ■実践編■ / ■応用編■」を `###` 見出し化
- 各章を箇条書き `-` に変換
- 各節（1.1 など）をネストした箇条書きに変換
- 表記ゆれとして「実際の３つ」を「実際の3つ」に統一

必要なら次に、章タイトルをリンク付き目次（アンカーリンク）にもできます。

_**Agent (default)**_

`@src/content/posts/2026-04_認証と認可 Keycloak入門 第2版.md` の該当箇所（`- IAM`）に、以下の内容を追記しました。

- 認証・認可以外のIAM機能
  - IDライフサイクル管理（JML）
  - ユーザー情報統合（LDAP/AD連携）
  - 特権アクセス管理（PAM）
  - 監査ログ・証跡管理
  - セルフサービス化による運用効率化
- 押さえるべき実務ポイント
  - 最小権限・RBAC
  - 定期的な権限棚卸し
  - SoD（職務分掌）統制
  - 可用性/DR対策
  - 法令・規制対応

必要なら、次にこの部分を「Keycloakでどこまで実現できるか（できる/外部製品が必要）」の観点で整理して追記できます。

_**Agent (default)**_

`@src/content/posts/2026-04_認証と認可 Keycloak入門 第2版.md` の `#### KeyCloakの中のIAM機能` 配下に、Keycloakが担うIAM機能を追記しました。

- ID管理（ユーザー/グループ/ロール、LDAP/AD連携）
- 認証（ログイン・MFA・認証フロー）
- 認可（RBAC、スコープ、ポリシーベース認可）
- フェデレーション/SSO（OIDC/SAML、外部IdP、SLO）
- トークン/セッション制御（発行・更新・失効）
- 監査/運用管理（イベントログ、Admin API、Realm運用）

必要なら次に、同じ箇所へ「Keycloak単体でカバーしにくい領域（例: PAMや承認ワークフロー）」も追記できます。

`@src/content/posts/2026-04_認証と認可 Keycloak入門 第2版.md` の `71-95` 相当部分を、以下の形で更新しました。

- Keycloakが担うIAM機能を**テーブル形式**に変換
- 追加で `#### KeyCloakの守備範囲外（または単体では不足しやすい）IAM機能` を新設

これで「Keycloakでできること」と「外部製品が必要になりやすいこと」が比較しやすくなっています。  
必要なら次に、あなたの利用想定（例: 社内向けB2E/B2B/B2C）に合わせて、どの外部製品候補が現実的かを絞り込んで追記できます。

_**Agent (default)**_

`@src/content/posts/2026-04_認証と認可 Keycloak入門 第2版.md` の該当箇所を、`mermaid` の `timeline` 形式で置き換えました。

- 2013-07: GitHub初公開
- 2022: Keycloak 17で `WildFly → Quarkus` へ刷新
- 2023-04: CNCF Incubating 承認

この形式なら、Keycloakの進化ポイント（公開→刷新→CNCF）の流れが一目で追えるようになっています。

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="web_search">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **web_search**</summary>


Parameters:

```json

  "searchTerm": "Keycloak official website latest news Quarkus distribution support"
}
```
Result:

```json
{
  "isFinal": true,
  "references": [
    {
      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [Keycloak 26.5.4 released - Keycloak](https://www.keycloak.org/2026/02/keycloak-2654-released)\n2. [Migrating to Quarkus distribution - Keycloak](https://www.keycloak.org/migration/migrating-to-quarkus)\n3. [Keycloak 25.0.0 released - Keycloak](https://www.keycloak.org/2024/06/keycloak-2500-released.html)\n4. [Keycloak 26.5.0 released - Keycloak](https://www.keycloak.org/2026/01/keycloak-2650-released)\n5. [Release Notes](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/release_notes/)\n\n# Keycloak Latest News and Quarkus Distribution Support\n\n## Latest Release\n\nKeycloak 26.5.4 was released on February 20, 2026.[1] This version includes multiple security fixes (CVE-2026-1190, CVE-2026-0707, CVE-2025-5416, CVE-2026-2575, and CVE-2026-2733), enhancements, and bug fixes.[1]\n\n## Quarkus Distribution Support\n\nKeycloak has transitioned to a Quarkus-based distribution as its default starting with Keycloak 17.[2] The Quarkus distribution replaces the legacy WildFly distribution and offers several key differences:\n\n- **Configuration**: Uses simple configuration files, CLI arguments, and environment variables instead of complex XML files[2]\n- **Build Process**: Requires a separate build step for optimization through an augmentation process, rather than runtime deployment like WildFly[2]\n- **Container Benefits**: Brings immutability to containers, faster startup, and more predictability[2]\n- **Custom Providers**: Packaged and deployed differently than in the WildFly distribution[2]\n\nThe Quarkus distribution also introduced a new Kubernetes operator with Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs).[2]\n\n## Migration Path\n\nFor users migrating from WildFly to Quarkus, the Keycloak website provides a dedicated migration guide that explains the configuration changes and how to adapt existing setups to the new distribution.[2]",
      "title": "Web search results for query: Keycloak official website latest news Quarkus distribution support"
    }
  ],
  "rejected": false
}
```
User decision: **accepted**

Status: **completed**

```json
{
  "isFinal": true,
  "references": [
    {
      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [Keycloak joins CNCF as an incubating project | CNCF](https://www.cncf.io/blog/2023/04/11/keycloak-joins-cncf-as-an-incubating-project/)\n2. [Keycloak, Cloud Native IAM For Cloud Native Applications, Joins The CNCF - TFiR](https://tfir.io/keycloak-cloud-native-iam-for-cloud-native-applications-joins-the-cncf)\n3. [Keycloak | CNCF](https://www.cncf.io/projects/keycloak/)\n4. [Keycloak | CNCF Presentations](https://presentations.cncf.io/projects/keycloak/)\n5. [New Gold Member Hitachi Joins the Cloud Native Computing Foundation](https://www.cncf.io/announcements/2023/04/17/new-gold-member-hitachi-joins-the-cloud-native-computing-foundation/)\n\n# Keycloak Joins CNCF as Incubating Project\n\nOn April 10, 2023, the CNCF Technical Oversight Committee voted to accept Keycloak as a CNCF incubating project.[1][3]\n\n## What is Keycloak\n\nKeycloak is an open-source Identity and Access Management (IAM) solution that provides centralized authentication and authorization for applications and APIs.[1] It runs as a single lightweight container image and integrates with Kubernetes through Operators, making it suitable for cloud native deployments.[1]\n\n## Project Background\n\nCreated in 2014 by Bill Burke and Stian Thorgersen, Keycloak has been in production for over eight years and is used by major organizations including Accenture, CERN, Cisco, Hitachi, and Okta.[1] At the time of acceptance, the project had achieved 15,000+ GitHub stars, 100+ releases, 855 contributors, and over 21,000 issues.[1]\n\n## Key Features\n\nThe project was refactored to run on the Quarkus container-optimized Java runtime, reducing boot time by more than 50% and enabling Keycloak to spin up in less than five seconds.[2] It integrates well with the cloud native ecosystem, supporting Prometheus metrics and integrating with CNCF projects like Argo, Envoy, Jaeger, and Kubernetes.[1]\n\n## Future Focus\n\nProject maintainers plan to focus on usability and operational aspects, making it easier to deploy and operate Keycloak at scale in a cloud native way.[1]",
      "title": "Web search results for query: CNCF Keycloak incubating announcement April 2023"
    }
  ],
  "rejected": false
}
```

```json
      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [Keycloak Operator Installation - Keycloak](https://keycloak.org/operator/installation)\n2. [Operator Guide | Red Hat build of Keycloak | 22.0 | Red Hat Documentation](https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_build_of_keycloak/22.0/html-single/operator_guide/index)\n3. [Advanced configuration - Keycloak](https://keycloak.org/operator/advanced-configuration)\n4. [keycloak/keycloak-operator](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-operator)\n5. [keycloak/operator at main · keycloak/keycloak · GitHub](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/tree/main/operator)\n\n# Keycloak Operator Kubernetes Official Documentation\n\n## Installation Methods\n\nThe Keycloak Operator can be installed on Kubernetes and OpenShift through two primary methods:[1]\n\n**1. Operator Lifecycle Manager (OLM)** - The recommended approach for Kubernetes environments.[1] Prerequisites include having OLM installed and cluster-admin permissions. On OpenShift, you can install via the web console by navigating to Home \u003e Operators \u003e OperatorHub, searching for \"keycloak-operator,\" and selecting the &#96;fast&#96; channel.[1]\n\n**2. kubectl without OLM** - For vanilla Kubernetes clusters, you can install using kubectl commands to apply Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) directly.[1]\n\n## Important Upgrade Consideration\n\nRed Hat strongly recommends configuring **manual approval mode** for OLM upgrades rather than using automatic updates.[1] This is because automatic upgrades can cause unintended Keycloak version upgrades, compatibility issues with existing Keycloak CR configurations, and database migration problems with no downgrade option.[1] Manual approval allows you to review release notes, schedule maintenance windows, and test upgrades before applying them.[1]\n\n## Advanced Configuration\n\nThe Keycloak CR (Custom Resource) exposes many server options as first-class fields. For options not exposed as dedicated fields, you can use the &#96;additionalOptions&#96; field to include any available configuration as key-value pairs or Secret object references.[3] Custom environment variables can also be set via the &#96;spec.env&#96; field.[3]\n\n## Documentation Resources\n\nOfficial documentation is available at keycloak.org, including guides on installation, basic deployment, and advanced configuration.[1][3] The operator source code is maintained in the main Keycloak repository.[5]",
      "title": "Web search results for query: Keycloak Operator Kubernetes official documentation"
    }
  ],
  "rejected": false
}
```

- Quarkus前提運用（Keycloak 17以降）
- Kubernetes/Operator運用の要点
- Operator更新を手動承認にする重要性

@src/content/posts/2026-04_認証と認可 Keycloak入門 第2版.md:114-115 
Quarkusについての概要記載と、他のJavaアプリケーションのフレームワークとの比較表を記載して、歴代のものも含めて

---

_**Agent (default)**_

`### Quakusについて` セクションに、Quarkusの概要と主要Javaフレームワークの比較（歴代含む）を追記します。まず周辺の見出し構造を確認して整合させます。

反映しました。`@src/content/posts/2026-04_認証と認可 Keycloak入門 第2版.md` の `### Quakusについて` を以下の内容で拡充しています。

- 見出しを `### Quarkusについて` に修正
- Quarkusの概要（なぜクラウドネイティブで強いか）を追記
- KeycloakがQuarkus移行した背景を文脈として追記
- 主な特徴（高速起動・省メモリ・Kubernetes親和性・JVM/Native）を箇条書き化
- 「歴代含む」Javaフレームワーク比較表を追加  
  （Java EE/Jakarta EE, Struts, Spring, Play, Dropwizard, Micronaut, Helidon, Spring Boot, Quarkus）
